topics: Find the sum of two sparse polynomials using arrays
Find the transpose of a sparse matrix and sum of two sparse matrices
Convert infix expression to postfix (or prefix) and then evaluate using stack
Implement Queue, Dequeue, and Circular Queue using arrays
Implement backward and forward navigation of visited web pages in a web browser using doubly linked list operations
Implement addition and multiplication of polynomials using singly linked lists
Create a binary tree for a given simple arithmetic expression and find the prefix / postfix equivalent
Implement a dictionary of word-meaning pairs using binary search trees
Find the shortest distance of every cell from a landmine inside a maze
Solve the water pouring problem (10-litre, 7-litre, and 4-litre containers) modeled as a graph problem
Implement the find and replace feature in a text editor
Implement an efficient search algorithm for a sorted array
Implement Bubble Sort, Insertion Sort, Radix Sort, Quick Sort, and Merge Sort and compare the number of steps involved
Generate the possible sequence of completion for customer categories (Defence personnel, Differently abled, Senior citizen, Ordinary) using priority
Implement a spell checker using a hash table to store a dictionary of words for fast lookup with suggestions for misspelled words
Simulation of a basic memory allocator and garbage collector using doubly linked list
Find the maximum activity points from at most k events using array
Merge K sorted lists into a single sorted list using a min-heap

Introduction to Linux and usage of terminal editors (man, ls, echo, read, more, less, cd, mkdir, find, mv, tar, wc, paste, head, tail, grep, expr, chmod, chown, redirections and piping, useradd, usermod, userdel, passwd, df, ps, ssh, ssh-keygen, ssh-copy-id)
Study of Shell scripting aspects: bash syntax, environment variables, variables, control constructs (if, for, while), aliases and functions, and accessing command line arguments
Study of startup scripts, login and logout scripts, and familiarity with systemd and system V init scripts
Write a script to create a directory structure and implement a script to list all files and directories within a specified directory showing date of creation and serial number
Write a script to automate a task and schedule it using cron, and create a script to clean up old log files periodically
Write a script to monitor system resources (CPU, memory, and disk usage) and implement a script to send an alert if resource usage exceeds a specified threshold

Study of IPv4 networking, command line tools, and network commands (ping, traceroute, nslookup, tcpdump), configuring IP addresses (dynamic and static), subnet masks, CIDR address schemes, iptables, LAN firewall configuration, and application layer (L7) proxies
Write a script to ping a list of servers and log the results, and implement a script to check if a specific port is open on a remote server
Write a script to automate common network troubleshooting commands and write a script to capture network packets using tcpdump
Write a script to start, stop, and restart network services (Apache, Nginx, MySQL) and implement a script to check the status of these services and restart them if not running
Write a script to set up basic firewall rules using iptables or firewalld, and implement a script to allow or block specific IP addresses or ranges
Familiarizing Python: variables, decision statements, iteration statements, and functions
Write a Python program to check the strength of a password
Write a keylogger / key logger detection tool program using Python
Write a Python program to implement primality testing using Miller-Rabin method
Write a Python program to implement pseudo-random number generation
Implement client-server communication using socket programming and TCP as transport layer protocol
Implement client-server communication using socket programming and UDP as transport layer protocol
